reasoning: There is currently no approved vaccine for Onchocerciasis (River blindness). The most advanced vaccine candidates (such as Ov-103 and Ov-RAL-2) were only expected to enter Phase I clinical trials in 2022. Vaccine development pathways typically take 10+ years from Phase I to regulatory approval and mass deployment. Therefore, it is virtually impossible for a vaccine to be developed, approved, and updated on the relevant Wikipedia page by October 2025. I am placing the probabilities at the minimum allowed bounds (0.05-0.06), with a slight increase over time to account for the infinitesimally small chance of either an accelerated breakthrough or a Wikipedia vandalism/misinformation event.
